meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup for Non-Coders (SaaS Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop flying blind. Learn how to implement professional analytics for your SaaS without coding experience using Antigravity and Claude Code. Start today.
content.answer_block: You don't need to be a developer to track user behavior. By leveraging AI-assisted workflows like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can inject robust tracking snippets into your stack, visualize conversion funnels, and make data-driven decisions that turn your MVP into a revenue-generating machine. Data is your ultimate competitive edge.
content.intro:
Most founders treat their SaaS like a black box. You ship features, hope for the best, and wonder why the churn rate feels like a leaking bucket. Without analytics, you aren't building a business; you are gambling with your runway. The 'no-code' barrier is a myth—you just need the right leverage.

Today, we bridge the gap between intent and insight. Using modern AI engineering tools, we will strip away the complexity of manual instrumentation. You will move from 'guessing what users want' to 'knowing exactly where they convert.' Let’s turn your data into your most valuable asset.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single action that indicates user value (e.g., 'Project Created' or 'API Key Generated').
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Product Lead. Analyze my landing page and app flow to suggest the 3 most critical user events I should track to measure product-market fit. Format as a JSON event schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my current project architecture and suggest where to place tracking events for user onboarding.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate a provider like PostHog or Mixpanel. We will use Claude Code to inject the initialization script into your app's head/layout component.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular initialization script for PostHog. Include logic for identifying users and tracking page views automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Inject the PostHog initialization script into the main layout file and ensure it uses environment variables for the API key.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Actions
steps.description: Wrap your primary CTA buttons with event tracking calls. This tracks the 'intent' rather than just the page load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable wrapper function for tracking events. It should handle event names and metadata properties securely.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scan my codebase for primary buttons and wrap their onClick handlers with the tracking function created in step 2.'

steps.title: Validate Data Flow
steps.description: Ensure the events are actually firing. We use AI to verify the network requests in the browser console.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a checklist for validating analytics events using the browser Network tab. How do I verify the payload structure matches my schema?
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Provide a script to simulate a user session that triggers all my tracked events for testing purposes.'

steps.title: Build Your Founder Dashboard
steps.description: Create a high-level view that tracks signups, active users, and conversion rates without needing SQL knowledge.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a layout for a founder-focused dashboard. What are the 4 key charts I should display on the home screen to monitor business health?
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a dashboard component that pulls data from the analytics API and renders a simple bar chart of daily active users.'

pro_tips: Always use environment variables for your API keys; never hardcode them in your repo, even if you are a solo founder.
Track 'Negative Events'—like a user closing a modal without taking action—to identify friction points in your UI.
Don't track everything. Focus on a maximum of 5 events initially to avoid 'data noise' that leads to analysis paralysis.
Use distinct user IDs from your auth provider (e.g., Clerk or Auth0) to maintain consistency across devices.
Automate your reporting by setting up a weekly summary email from your analytics tool to your inbox—don't wait for a dip to check the data.
content.next_steps: Once your events are flowing, set up a 15-minute weekly 'Data Review' on your calendar. Use this time to compare your analytics against your revenue goals.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to transform your SaaS from a guess-based project into a data-driven powerhouse. Stop building in the dark and start optimizing for growth today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
